note: perf v0.3 — compile_s included; B=1, image_size=224, num_image_tokens=128, seq_len=136. Baseline graph_break_count=28 (graph_count=29) — matches corpus eval count of 19 distinct break_reasons + repeats from MoE expert loop and capture_outputs lock.
interpretation: Compiled is ~15% SLOWER than eager. Unlike Mistral3 where Inductor still wins despite breaks, here the 28 fragments + MoE per-expert loop (sequential_experts_gemm) prevent any meaningful fusion — compile-time dominates and per-expert kernel-launch overhead from the broken trace exceeds eager's overhead. Discovery should improve fix-quality (fewer breaks, fullgraph closer to True) AND can plausibly flip speedup back above 1.0 if the MoE loop folds into a single graph. Compare against compiled_ms=15.32 ms and graph_break_count=28.
